title: Rice Per Person Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Calculate Rice Portions: Grams Per Person
description: Calculate how many grams of rice to cook per person based on meal type. Includes water ratios. Perfect for risotto, paella, sushi, and rice bowls.
intro: How much rice per person? It depends on whether rice is a **main dish** or **side dish**, the type of rice, and whether you're cooking for kids. This calculator tells you the **grams of uncooked rice** and **water needed** for perfectly cooked rice—no leftovers.
keyTakeaway: **As a side dish**: 60–75 g raw rice per person. **As a main dish** (risotto, paella): 100–120 g. **Water ratio**: 2 cups water per 1 cup rice (white rice).
useCases: You're cooking rice for 6 people and want to get the portion size right.
You want to avoid having leftover or running short on rice.
You need to calculate rice for a big event or dinner party.
You want to know how much water to add based on rice amount.
You need to adjust portions for kids.

faq.q: How much uncooked rice per person?
faq.a: For a **side dish**: 60–75 grams per adult. For a **main dish** (risotto or paella): 100–120 grams. Kids typically eat half those amounts.
**White rice**: 2 cups water per 1 cup rice. **Brown rice**: 2.5 cups water per 1 cup rice. **Basmati**: 1.5 cups water per 1 cup rice. Ratios vary slightly based on your pot and heat.

faq.q: How much does rice expand when cooked?
faq.a: Rice typically **doubles to triples** in weight: 100 grams raw rice yields about 250–300 grams cooked. Brown rice yields slightly less.

faq.q: How much sushi rice per person?
faq.a: Plan for **100–120 grams raw rice per person** for sushi (yields more rolls than you'd expect). Each 8-piece roll needs about 90 grams cooked sushi rice.

faq.q: How do I calculate rice for 20 people?
faq.a: For 20 people as a side dish: 20 × 70g = **1,400g** (about 3 pounds) of raw rice. That's roughly 7 cups rice with 14 cups water.

faq.q: Do kids need smaller rice portions?
faq.a: Yes. Children ages 3–10 eat **half the adult portion**: 30–40g raw rice as a side dish. This calculator auto-adjusts if you select 'yes' for children.

faq.q: Is it safe to reheat cooked rice?
faq.a: Rice can harbor **Bacillus cereus** if left at room temperature over 1 hour. Cool it quickly, refrigerate, and reheat to over 160°F (70°C). Don't leave cooked rice out.

faq.q: How many cups of rice do I need for 4 people?
faq.a: For 4 adults as a side dish: about 1.5–1.75 cups raw rice. For a main dish: 1.8–2.2 cups. Use the calculator above for exact amounts.

faq.q: Should I rinse rice before cooking?
faq.a: **Yes for white rice** (removes excess starch). **No for risotto rice** (you need the starch for creaminess). Brown and basmati benefit from a quick rinse.

explanation:
## How Much Rice Per Person

The portion size depends on whether rice is the **main dish** or a **side dish**, the rice variety (long grain, risotto, carnaroli), and whether you're serving children. As a rule: **70–80g raw rice** per adult for main courses, **40–50g** for side dishes.

## Raw Rice Grams Per Person: Quick Reference

| Meal Type | Adult | Child (6–12 years) |
|---|---|---|
| Main dish (risotto, paella) | 80 g | 50 g |
| Main dish without sides | 70 g | 45 g |
| Side dish | 50 g | 30 g |
| With heavy sauce/protein | 60 g | 40 g |
| Rice salad | 40 g | 25 g |

## Helpful Conversions

- **1 cup (200 ml)** ≈ 180 grams raw rice
- **Rice-to-water ratio**: 1:2 (white rice), 1:2.5 (brown rice), 1:3–3.5 (risotto, creamy risotto)
- **Cooking yield**: Rice triples in weight: 80g raw = ~240g cooked

## Common Mistakes & Pro Tips

- **Parboiled rice**: Use 1:1.5 water ratio instead of 1:2
- **Don't rinse risotto rice**: You need the starch for creaminess
- **Let it rest**: Cover and rest for 5 minutes after cooking (adds ~10% to final yield)
- Planning a big meal? Check out our [birthday party budget calculator](/calculadora-presupuesto-cumpleanos)

example.title: Real Example: Risotto for 4 Adults
example.result: For risotto serving 4 adults, use 320 grams rice and about 960 ml broth.
